properties.shortDescription: Cirkewwa has many popular dive sites in Malta: Cirkewwa Arch, Patrol Boat P29 & Tugboat Rozi wrecks, Susie's Pool, Madonna statue and wall reef with swimthroughs.
properties.links: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%C4%8Airkewwa http://www.mta.com.mt/loadfile.ashx?id=b64f30ec-c443-422f-8a69-afd84f03e76b|Malta Tourism Authority - Dive Site Report 14: Cirkewwa – Marfa Point (PDF)

properties.shortDescription: Patrol Boat P29 (Boltenhagen) is a popular wreck dive in Malta. P29 was 52m patrol boat that was scuttled 2007 in Cirkewwa. Wreck is at 34m depth.
Tugboat Rozi is a popular wreck dive in Malta. Rozi was 35m tug boat that was scuttled in 1992. The wreck rests off shore from Cirkewwa with max depth 34m.
Cirkewwa Arch dive site is a beautiful natural underwater archway in Malta, 12 metres below the surface and 8 metres above the seabed.
Paradise Bay reef dive in Cirkewwa offers rock formations, boulder fields, sandy bottoms, sea grass, drop-offs, swim-throughs and natural rock arch.
Susie's Pool is shallow diving area in Cirkewwa, often used to practice skills. Halfway along reef wall there is Madonna statue in small cave.
Reef Beyond Rozi is a nice wall reef with caves and overhangs far behind tugboat Rozi wreck and Cirkewwa Arch. Challenging dive site with max depth over 40m.
Ghar Lapsi has many shallow dive sites: 40m cave system, reefs with caves & walls (Finger Reef, Middle Reef, Black John), and underwater nativity scene (Crib).
properties.links: http://www.mta.com.mt/loadfile.ashx?id=2deea69a-7921-4887-9c74-8198dec7896c|Malta Tourism Authority - Dive Site Report 9: Finger Reef & Crib and Middle Reef (PDF)

properties.shortDescription: Ghar Lapsi Cave dive site is 40m long shallow cave system in Malta with lots of cracks that allow plenty of sunlight to seek in making the cave full of color.
Finger Reef dive site in Ghar Lapsi is finger shaped reef with small wall, and cave with hole in roof, which one can exit through. Max depth of dive is 20m.
Middle Reef consists of several reefs, surrounded by sand, sea grass & small boulders. Middle Reef is shallowest dive site in Ghar Lapsi, with max depth 12m.
Crib dive site in Ghar Lapsi is an underwater nativity scene with almost life-sized figures. Crib is located under an overhang on the outer reef at 22m.
Black John dive site is offshore reef near Ghar Lapsi in Malta with max depth 38m. Black John offers divers some interesting rock formations and halocline.
properties.links: http://www.mta.com.mt/loadfile.ashx?id=be9591ed-0d6c-4525-b9f1-9d9856d26fa1|Malta Tourism Authority - Dive Site Report 10: Black John (PDF)

properties.shortDescription: Wied iz-Zurrieq is famous for Blue Grotto & Um El Faroud wreck dive. Other dive sites in Wied iz-Zurrieq are East & West Reef, caves, helmet statue and valley.
properties.links: https://www.skylinewebcams.com/en/webcam/malta/malta/qrendi/wied-iz-zurrieq.html|Wied iz-Zurrieq live cam @ SkylineWebcams http://www.mta.com.mt/loadfile.ashx?id=d47da59e-11b3-4a4b-ad48-bbde54c4e416|Malta Tourism Authority - Dive Site Report 8: East Reef, West Reef & Caves, and Um el Faroud (PDF)

properties.shortDescription: Um El Faroud is the best wreck dive in Malta. Um El Faroud was scuttled 1998 for scuba diving off Wied iz-Zurrieq. Wreck is at 36m.
West Reef & Caves dive site is in Wied iz-Zurrieq. West Reef has drop-offs, ledges, gullies, boulders and two caves, one named Bell Tower Cave.
East Reef is one of the dive sites in Wied iz-Zurrieq. East Reef is wall dive with max depth over 30m. Often excellent visibility and lots of marine life.
Valley is starting/ending point for shore dives in Wied iz-Zurrieq. Shallow inlet is excellent site for night diving and training dives. Lots of marine life.

The caverns have a lot to explore, but it's accessible only by boat or about a 40 minutes swim from the valley entry. As it's a popular tourist site, there is also a lot of boat traffic. As a shallow dive site it's also exposed to swell.
Valletta is capital of Malta, located on northeast coast. Dive sites in Valletta are World War II wreck HMS Maori in St. Elmo Bay, and Fort St. Elmo reef.
HMS Maori was WWII British destroyer that sunk in 1942. Maori is a shallow wreck dive in Valletta, Malta. Wreck is half buried in sand with max depth 16m.
properties.links: http://www.wrecksite.eu/wreck.aspx?97120 https://underwatermalta.org/discover/hms-maori/ http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HMS_Maori_%28F24%29 https://www.skylinewebcams.com/en/webcam/malta/malta/valletta/valletta-tigne-seafront.html|Valletta live cam (St. Elmo Bay on the left) @ SkylineWebcams http://www.mta.com.mt/loadfile.ashx?id=e04d489e-387d-434f-b8c9-31e194235a9a|Malta Tourism Authority - Dive Site Report 1: HMS Maori (PDF)

properties.shortDescription: Fort St. Elmo diving site is in Valletta, between Marsamxett and Grand Harbour, at the seaward side of the fort. This is a good reef dive with max depth 35m.
properties.links: http://www.mta.com.mt/loadfile.ashx?id=ee1498ce-4ca8-4928-91f8-8f5990b82b4c|Malta Tourism Authority - Dive Site Report 2: Fort St Elmo (PDF)

properties.shortDescription: Exiles is located in Sliema, northeastern Malta. The dive sites here are Tug 2 wreck, a tugboat scuttled in 2013, and Exiles reef. Max depth is 22m.
properties.links: http://www.mta.com.mt/loadfile.ashx?id=23ed7551-1b53-43ba-b531-af6b34a24483|Malta Tourism Authority - Dive Site Report 19: Exiles (PDF)

properties.shortDescription: Tug Boat 2 is shallow wreck dive off shore Exiles, Sliema, Malta. Tug 2 was 30m tugboat and was scuttled in 2013 for scuba diving. Wreck is upright at 22m.
Exiles Reef dive site in Sliema is a shallow reef with narrow gullies and overhangs, Posidonia seagrass fields and diversity of fish life. Max depth is 20m.
Fireworks barge wreck is in St. Julian’s Bay between Spinola and Exiles. Barge sank 2009 after fireworks explosion, and lies on sand at depth 10m.
properties.links: https://wrecksite.eu/wreck.aspx?228698 http://www.timesofmalta.com/articles/view/20090907/local/no-one-injured-in-st-julians-fireworks-barge-explosion.272514|Times of Malta article about the fireworks barge explosion http://www.independent.com.mt/articles/2009-09-09/news/sliema-fireworks-barge-sinks-263012/|Malta Independent article about the sinking of the barge

properties.shortDescription: Qawra Point dive site is on northeast coast of Malta. Diving in Qawra Point includes reefs, caves (Fra Ben Cave and L-Cave), small arch, and shallow bay.
properties.links: http://www.mta.com.mt/loadfile.ashx?id=426fecfd-ef92-4169-93f5-858873842f17|Malta Tourism Authority - Dive Site Report 17: Qawra Point North (PDF)

properties.shortDescription: Fra Ben Cave is shallow cavern dive on north side of Qawra Point. Fra Ben Cave entrance is 100m from entry point at 6m depth. Cavern has an open ceiling.
North Reef & Arch is offshore reef dive on north side of Qawra Point. Wall reef drops from 10m down to 15m. Near the reef there is little arch 18m deep.
Qawra Bay is shallow dive site on south east side of Qawra Point. This sheltered bay is suitable for training and try dives. Seabed is sand and seagrass.
L-Cave is deep swim-through cave dive off Qawra Point. L-Cave is quite tight L-shaped tunnel with sandy bottom located in small valley at depth around 33m.
Qawra Reef is offshore dive site off Qawra Point in Malta. Qawra Reef has nice wall with several big beautiful caves/caverns. Depth of dive from 15m to 40m.
Sliema is on northeast coast of Malta. Dive sites in Sliema are Fortizza Reef, Coral Gardens & Qui Si Sana, shallow reefs with caverns, tunnels, and gullies.
Fortizza Reef is shallow dive site in Sliema. Fortizza Reef has lots of caverns, tunnels, arches, overhangs, valleys, and gullies for divers to explore.
Coral Gardens is shallow dive site in Sliema. Coral Gardens reef has valleys and canyons with some interesting rock formations and swimthrough tunnels.
Qui Si Sana is a shallow reef dive site in Sliema. Qui Si Sana reef is mostly used for dive training and try dives.
Marsaskala is in south east Malta. Dive sites in Marsaskala are tugboat wrecks St. Michael & Tug 10 scuttled in 1998, Zonqor Point reefs, and Mini Blue Hole.
Tugboat 10 is shallow wreck dive in Marsaskala, Malta. Tug 10 is 16m tugboat scuttled 1998 for scuba diving. Wreck is upright at 21m depth.
St. Michael is shallow wreck dive in Marsaskala, Malta. St. Michael was 20m tugboat and was scuttled in 1998 for scuba diving. Wreck is upright at 22m.
P33 is 23m long former patrol boat that was scuttled for divers in Marsaskala 31.7.2021.
properties.links: https://timesofmalta.com/articles/view/Patrol-boat-to-be-scuttled.409388|Times of Malta: Patrol boat to be scuttled (3rd March 2012) https://era.org.mt/en/Documents/PA04687-10_Scuttling%20of%20P33%20off%20Gozo_Master%20EPS_FINAL.pdf|Environmental Planning Statement (April 2016)

properties.shortDescription: Mini Blue Hole dive site is in Marsaskala, Malta. The hole is a natural rock formation connected to the sea by an underwater tunnel. Shallow reefs as well.
properties.links: http://www.mta.com.mt/loadfile.ashx?id=4c3c180d-6419-4cce-8b9e-f8c380bb3517|Malta Tourism Authority - Dive Site Report 4: Mini Blue Hole (PDF)

properties.shortDescription: Zonqor Point dive site in Marsaskala has shallow reefs on both sides of the peninsula. Zonqor Point reefs are shore dives with max depth about 20m.
properties.links: http://www.mta.com.mt/loadfile.ashx?id=7b056076-bf8b-4393-baa7-e64886a799b4|Malta Tourism Authority - Dive Site Report 5: Zonqor Point North (PDF)

properties.shortDescription: Delimara Point is located in the south east corner of the main island overlooking the Malta Freeport.
East reef is the easiest and shallow dive location in Delimara Point, but accessible only on very calm weathers as it's exposed to swells.
South reef provides the most interesting wall/reef formations in the Delimara area. Be aware of the boat traffic on the area, DSMB is highly recommended on this dive site.
Delimara Cave is a nice chimney style cave located in the south reef. It's definitely something worth checking out when diving in the area.
Delimara Wreck is a decent size sunken and broken up barge in a pretty shallow waters.
Anchor Bay aka Popeye Village is shore diving site in Malta. Anchor Bay’s best dive is Scorpion Cave, large shallow cavern.
Scorpion Cave is the best dive site in Anchor Bay. Shallow cave with large entrance is suitable also for less experienced divers.
Anchor Bay dive site features a large anchor and its anchor chain. The anchor is about 3m in length, and lies on sand around 8m deep.
Anchor Bay reef is varied dive site with large boulders, swim-throughs, rocky reefs, sea grass meadows and rich marine life.
Anchor Bay training area is shallow sheltered place ideal for try dives and first course dives.
L'Ahrax Point is located on the northernmost point of the main island Malta.
There is a small cavern/cave on the very tip of L'Ahrax Point. It can be easily found by following the shore line.

L'Ahrax Point North Reef provides a long reef with a lots to explore; Rocky boulders, small canyons, swim throughs and sandy posidonia. It's a bit far from the shore entry, so ideally dived off a boat.
The tunnel provides access to the inland sea of L'Ahrax Point. It's open to the surface, so it's accessible by snorkelers also.
Inland Sea dive site in L'Ahrax Point features a cavern with collapsed roof leaving a large circular opening in the rock faces that rises 10m above sea surface.
Large Admiralty Anchor at Armier Bay
Sirens dive site is in St. Paul’s Bay in northern Malta. Sirens has shallow reef with max depth 12m and is mainly used for try dives and diving courses.
Harq Hammiem Cave in St. Julian's is the only known fully submerged terrestrial cavern in Malta, consisting of two chambers, with max depth 52m.
properties.links: http://www.maltatoday.com.mt/printversion/66484/#.WMwT46K1suV|MaltaToday's article about construction plans around the cave https://www.mepa.org.mt/cave

properties.shortDescription: Dornier Wal XI is seaplane wreckage in Gnejna Bay. Dornier Do J was German flying boat that crash landed 1932. Plane wreck is 12-15m deep.
SS Margit (MV Odile) is shallow wreck dive in Malta. Margit was a 100m passenger ship that sunk in 1941 in Kalkara Creek. Wreck is at 22m and quite broken.
Slugs Bay is shallow reef dive site in Mellieha Bay in northeast Malta. Slugs Bay has abundance of marine life and a natural rock arch. Max depth 12m.
properties.links: http://www.mta.com.mt/loadfile.ashx?id=0e84bc6f-7d5f-4c08-9278-9f962b387a8e|Malta Tourism Authority - Dive Site Report 16: Slugs Bay (PDF)

properties.shortDescription: Merkanti Reef is shallow dive site located off St. Julian’s in Malta. Merkanti Reef has many small overhangs and crevices with a variety of marine life.
properties.links: http://www.mta.com.mt/loadfile.ashx?id=69632061-c45d-4614-8b9f-aef51a222556|Malta Tourism Authority - Dive Site Report 18: Merkanti Reef (PDF) http://www.maltastartpages.com/diving/diving-sites/sliema-merkanti-reef/

properties.shortDescription: X127 is one of the WWII wreck dives around Malta. X127 was British water lighter that sunk 1942. Wreck is in Marsamxett Harbour off Manoel Island at 22m.
Migra Ferha dive site is under Dingli Cliffs in Malta. Migra Ferha has car wrecks, boulders, caves, caverns and overhangs.
Benghisa Point dive site is on south coast of Malta. Benghisa Point is shallow reef dive with some interesting rock formations including an arch. Depth 4-20m.
Not much to see on this dive site called Xrobb Il-Ghagin. Mainly just some nice boulders to explore with an easy entry to the water.
Mtahleb provides some nice caverns and large boulders to explore, but usually divers prefer to dive Migrah Ferha in this area.
HMS Jersey is WWII wreckage in Malta. Jersey was British destroyer that sunk 1941 in Valletta harbour. After clearance work only some remains left of wreck.
Tent Peg Wreck is old cargo steamer in Marsamxett Harbour between Manoel Island and Ta'Xbiex. Wreck is named after tent pegs cargo, not true shipwreck but wreckage site.
Ricasoli Cannons dive site is in Kalkara, Malta near Fort Ricasoli. There are three cannons in a cavern 2-3m deep. The shallow reef dive has max depth 12m.
Xghajra Reef is labyrinth of small tunnels and caves with low arches. This shore dive site is located east side of Xghajra village. Max depth of reef is 18m.
Bristol Beaufighter is WWII airplane wreck dive in Malta. Beaufighter sunk in 1943 due to mechanical problems. Plane wreck is upside down at 38m depth.
HMS Hellespont is WWII wreck dive in Malta. She was sunk 1942 during air raid and scuttled outside Valletta after the war. Hellespont wreck is at 41m deep.
properties.links: http://www.wrecksite.eu/wreck.aspx?97342|Wrecksite.eu - HMAV Hellespont https://sketchfab.com/models/c8922047f2fd409c93b823f2760b144f|3D model of HMS Hellespont wreck

properties.shortDescription: HM Drifter Eddy is one of the WWII wreck dives in Malta. Eddy sunk after hitting a mine in 1942. 27m long drifter wreck lies outside Valletta at 56m depth.
HMS St. Angelo is WWII wreck dive in Malta. St. Angelo was a tugboat used as a minesweeper, hit a mine and sunk in 1942. Wreck is upright at a depth of 54m.
Schnellboot S-31 is WWII wreck dive in Malta. S-31 was German S-boot that hit mine off Valletta and sunk 1942. Wreck is 65m deep.
properties.links: https://www.wrecksite.eu/wreck.aspx?160992|Wrecksite.eu - S-31 Schnellboot https://heritagemalta.org/schnellboot/|Heritage Malta - Schnellboot https://underwatermalta.org/discover/schnellboot/|Underwater Malta Virtual Museum - Schnellboot S-31 https://sketchfab.com/models/c32d5aed4734407cbbb8490ab1835b0b|3D model of S-31

properties.shortDescription: HMS Southwold is WWII wreck dive in Malta. Southwold was sunk by mine 1942. Wreck is in two parts, bow 68m and stern 73m deep.
properties.links: http://wrecksite.eu/wreck.aspx?30773 (bow)|Wrecksite.eu - HMS Southwold's bow section http://wrecksite.eu/wreck.aspx?164390 (aft)|Wrecksite.eu - HMS Southwold's aft section http://www.calypsosac.org/hms-southwold/ https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HMS_Southwold_(L10)|Wikipedia.org - HMS Southwold (L10) https://heritagemalta.org/hms-southwold/|Heritage Malta - HMS Southwold https://sketchfab.com/models/ecf118bfc255459c93546a0ed1e5dc99|3D model of HMS Southwold's bow section

properties.shortDescription: Le Polynesien is WWI wreck dive in Malta. SS Polynesien was 152m French passenger ship sunk by torpedo in 1918. Poly wreck max depth is 65m.
properties.links: http://www.wrecksite.eu/wreck.aspx?124537|Wrecksite.eu - SS Polynesien http://www.messageries-maritimes.org/polynes.htm|L'Encyclopedie des Messageries Maritimes - Le Polynesien https://sketchfab.com/3d-models/ss-le-polynesien-e637b63fde90470e8ae4562cf43d80b7|3D model of Le Polynesien wreck

properties.shortDescription: Barge wreck is situated off St. Paul’s Bay, with max depth about 60m. Wreck is quite large barge where divers can enter engine room and accommodation area.
Benghisa Reef dive site is 1km off Benghisa Point on south coast of Malta, stretching 1.5km southeast. Benghisa Reef starts from 10m and drops down to 50m.
Scotscraig is wreck dive in Malta. MV Scotscraig was small river ferry, later used in Popeye movie, now boat dive at 21m. Wreck is located near Anchor Bay.
Qammieh Point aka Devil's Reef is a nice wall dive site with few caves and caverns, starting from a shallow plateau and reaching depths well beyond 50m.
HMS Stubborn is deep submarine wreck in Malta. Stubborn was 66m British submarine scuttled as sonar training target 1946. Wreck is at 56m depth.
MV Imperial Eagle is deep wreck dive in Malta. Imperial Eagle was 45m long ferry, scuttled in 1999 off Qawra Point. Wreck is upright on at depth 42m.
Kristu tal-Bahhara (Christ of the Sailors) dive site is underwater statue of Jesus Christ. Statue was sunk 1990 and relocated off Qawra in 2000. Depth max 35m.
Ras ir-Raheb dive site has nice cave starting from surface and reaching down to 25m. Rock wall is nearly vertical, sloping down to sandy seabed over 70m deep.
Bristol Blenheim Bomber is WWII airplane wreck dive in Malta. Blenheim was shot in one engine and ditched in the sea in 1941. Plane wreck is at 42m depth.
Levant II was built 1904 and sunk 1km off Valletta 1952. Cable layer wreck was rediscovered 1999 and identified as Levant II 10 years later. Max depth is 59m.
HMS Russell is WWI wreck in Malta. Russell was British battleship built 1901. She hit mines and sank 1916. Wreck is at 115m depth.
Mosquito Fighter Bomber is airplane wreck dive in Malta. De Havilland Mosquito crashed in sea due to engine problems in 1949. Plane wreck is 40m deep.
MV Pippo is wreck dive off Ahrax Point. Pippo took in water and sank 2004 on its way from Comino to Malta. Small wreck is boat dive at depth 35m on sand.
HMS Kingston is WWII wreckage in Malta.  Kingston was British destroyer that sunk at docks in 1942, later used as blockship near Selmun and scrapped there.
HMS Nasturtium is WWI wreck dive in Malta. Nasturtium was British minesweeping sloop that hit mine and sank in 1916. Wreck is 68m deep.
Cargo Ship Camadan is technical wreck dive in Malta. MV Camadan was Turkish 80m long cargo ship that flooded and floundered in 2002. Wreck is at 90m depth.
Luciston Collier is WWI wreck dive in Malta. SS Luciston was British coal cargo ship (collier) torpedoed 1916. Wreck is at 104m depth.
B-24 Liberator Bomber is WWII airplane wreck dive in Malta. B-24 Liberator ditched into sea 1943. Bomber wreck is 55m deep.
Dredger Anadrian is technical wreck dive in Malta. Anadrian was 40m long grab dredger built in 1952. Wreck is located outside of Bahar ic-Caghaq 65m deep.
Lockheed P2V Neptune is 2015 discovered airplane wreck dive site in Malta at depth of 30m. Aircraft was damaged during landing and used for filming in 1950s.
HMD Trusty Star is WWII wreck dive in Malta. Trusty Star was British minesweeper that hit mine and sank 1942. Wreck is 90m deep.
Unknown Schnellboot
Ġebel ġol-Baħar is a possible megalithic temple located underwater off the coast of St. Julian's, Malta.
HMS Olympus is WWII submarine wreck in Malta. Olympus was British submarine that hit mine and sank 1942. Wreck is 130m deep.
HMY Aegusa is WWI wreck dive in Malta. Aegusa was luxury yacht used as patrol vessel, sank by mine in 1916. Wreck is at 76m depth.
HMS Andromeda is WWII wreck in Malta. The tug sunk in Grand Harbour of Valletta in 1942 during air raid. Andromeda was 27m long and wreck lies 20m deep.
ORP Kujawiak (HMS Oakley) is WWII wreck dive in Malta. Kujawiak was Polish escort destroyer that hit mine and sank 1942. Wreck is 100m deep.
Small caves about half way between Ghar Lapsi and Wied iz-Zurrieq
Junkers Ju 88 is WWII airplane wreck dive in Malta. Junkers 88 was German twin-engine bomber. Wreck is 55m deep off Bahar ic-Caghaq.
Stoker Cave dive site is under Dingli Cliffs on west coast of Malta. Ceiling has formations of stalactites & stalagmites. Stoker Cave is at 20-30m depth.
"Casper" is unknown wreck in Malta. Discovered 2014 by local dive centre and nicknamed Casper, this wreck lies 38m deep and is badly broken up and twisted.
Fairey Swordfish is WWII airplane wreck dive in Malta. Swordfish was ditched in the sea due engine problems in 1943. Plane wreck lies at 70m depth.
MV King Edwin is WWII wreck in Malta. King Edwin was British cargo ship that sank 1943 in Grand Harbour after fire. Wreck is 112m deep.
HMS Urge is WWII submarine wreck in Malta. Urge was 58m British submarine that hit a mine and sank in 1942. Wreck is about 130m deep.
properties.links: https://timesofmalta.com/articles/view/confirmed-wreck-off-malta-is-the-submarine-sunk-in-1942.870478|Times of Malta: Wreck off Malta is the submarine sunk in 1942 https://www.tvm.com.mt/en/news/wwii-mystery-solved-hms-urge-discovered-off-malta/|TVM: WWII mystery solved – HMS Urge discovered off Malta (30th October 2019) https://www.wrecksite.eu/wreck.aspx?16108 https://uboat.net/allies/warships/ship/3538.html https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HMS_Urge

properties.shortDescription: Junkers Ju 88 is WWII airplane wreck dive in Malta. Junkers 88 was German twin-engine bomber. Wreck is 106m deep off Marsaskala.
Douglas A-1 Skyraider is airplane wreck dive in Malta. Skyraider ditched into sea 1947. Attack aircraft wreck is 96m deep.

The Martin Maryland 167 was a light bomber with a three-member crew. The Maryland lies upside down on a sandy seabed at a depth of 70 metres off the coast of Marsaskala. The site was discovered during a side scan sonar survey in the spring of 2020.
Dwejra is the most popular scuba diving site in Gozo. Dive sites in Dwejra include Inland Sea & Tunnel, Azure Reef, Blue Hole, Coral Gardens & Cave and many more.
Inland Sea is popular dive site in Dwejra, Gozo. Tunnel is 80m long cave connecting Inland Sea to the Mediterranean, offering amazing scuba diving experience.
Blue Hole in Gozo is maybe the most beautiful dive of Maltese Islands. Blue Hole provides entry&exit for other dive sites in Dwejra like Azure Reef & Coral Gardens.
Azure Reef dive site in Gozo formed when Azure Window collapsed 2017. Reef starts just below surface and continues to over 40m depth.
Coral Cave dive site is located at Dwejra in western Gozo. Coral Cave is large cavern with colourful corals. Depth 20-30m.

properties.links: https://books.google.fi/books?id=Ld6QDgAAQBAJ&lpg=PP1&pg=PA116#v=onepage|Crocodile Rock - - Diving Gozo and Comino - Richard Salter https://maltasac.org/crocodile-rock-dwejra/ http://www.calypsosac.org/crocodile-rock/

properties.shortDescription: Tal-Barba dive site is located at Dwejra in western Gozo. Tal-Barba cave reaches 120m inwards and is also known as 200m Tunnel.
Whale Cave dive site is located at Dwejra in western Gozo. Whale Cave is a crack cave open to surface and bottom at 25m depth.
Xatt l-Ahmar is popular scuba dive site in Gozo. Dives in Xatt l-Ahmar are deep wrecks Cominoland, Karwela & Xlendi, wall reef with caves, and shallow bay.
MV Xlendi is deep wreck dive in Gozo. Xlendi was 80m ro-ro ferry and was scuttled for scuba diving in 1999. Xlendi wreck is upside down at 42m depth.
properties.links: http://www.wrecksite.eu/wreck.aspx?117736|Wrecksite.eu - M/S Xlendi http://www.calypsosac.org/mv-xlendi/|CSAC - MV Xlendi https://sketchfab.com/3d-models/3-gozo-wrecks-4a92c2fb7f264d45b46c9ca6253d7fae|3D model of Gozo wrecks

properties.shortDescription: MV Karwela is the most dived wreck in Gozo. Karwela was 50m passenger ferry and was scuttled for scuba diving in 2006. Wreck is upright at 40m depth.
properties.links: http://www.wrecksite.eu/wreck.aspx?128134|Wrecksite.eu - M/S Karwela http://www.calypsosac.org/karwela/|CSAC - Karwela https://sketchfab.com/3d-models/3-gozo-wrecks-4a92c2fb7f264d45b46c9ca6253d7fae|3D model of Gozo wrecks

properties.shortDescription: MV Cominoland is deep wreck dive in Gozo. Cominoland was 40m tourist cruise ship and was scuttled for scuba diving in 2006. Wreck is upright at 40m depth.
properties.links: http://www.wrecksite.eu/wreck.aspx?128135|Wrecksite.eu - M/S Cominoland http://www.calypsosac.org/cominoland/|CSAC - Cominoland https://sketchfab.com/3d-models/3-gozo-wrecks-4a92c2fb7f264d45b46c9ca6253d7fae|3D model of Gozo wrecks

properties.shortDescription: Xlendi Bay is located on southwest coast of Gozo. Dive sites in Xlendi Bay are beautiful shallow tunnel cave and pretty reef with lots of marine life.
properties.links: https://www.skylinewebcams.com/en/webcam/malta/gozo/munxar/gozo-munxar.html|Gozo - Munxar live cam (View of the Xlendi Bay) @ SkylineWebcams

properties.shortDescription: Xlendi Tunnel dive site is located in Xlendi Bay in Gozo. Beautiful shallow tunnel cave is 70m long with max depth 10m. Suitable for all levels of divers.
Xlendi Reef dive site is located in Xlendi Bay in Gozo. Shallower inner reef is max 12m, outer reef about 25m with a pinnacle reaching almost to the surface.
The park is made of different wrecks from old times.
Double Arch dive site in Gozo got its name from two natural rock arches on top of each other. Depth at Double Arch is from 15m to 45m and it's 200m offshore.
Ras il-Hobz aka Middle Finger is a pinnacle rising out from depths of 40+m to around 10m. The pinnacle attracts lots of sea life, like Jacks, Dentex and Moray eels.
Ghar id-dar is next to Wied Ir-Raheb in Gozo. Ghar id dar is a truely wonderful cave with air pockets and lots to explore!
Wied il-Mielah dive site in north Gozo has window/archway over the sea. For divers there are two beautiful caves and a swim through near a shallow reef.
Newwiela Point in southern Gozo has a shallow plateau surrounded by a steep drop-off and wall with large boulders reaching depths beyond 60 meters.
Dawra Tas-Sanap in Gozo has shallow reef with arch and cave. Deeper down the reef contains massive boulders and caverns ending at sandy bottom around 65m.
Zurziep Reef starts from a platform at depth of 5m. From there the site slopes down to 60+m offering few big boulders and small swim-throughs on the way.
Fungus Rock is a boat dive site at Dwejra in Gozo. Fungus Rock is an islet at the entrance of Dwejra Bay. This is a wall dive with depths over 60m.
The reef slowly slopes down from 6m to around 35m to a sandy bottom. There are four big anchors around the area.
Phoenician shipwreck is 2700 years old wreck near Xlendi Bay in Gozo at 110m depth which is also called a Tower Wreck. Phoenician shipwreck's cargo includes grinding stones and amphorae.
Hephaestus is an oil tanker that was scuttled to Xatt l-Ahmar on 29.8.2022 as an underwater attraction. Togo-registered Hephaestus is about 60 m in length with a beam of 8 m.

properties.shortDescription: The history of the Spitfire wreck located off the coast of Gozo is linked to Operation Husky – the Allied invasion of Sicily (10 July 1943-17 August 1943).The aircraft is located upright on a sandy seabed at a depth of 70 metres.
Patrol Boat P31 (Pasewalk) is the only wreck dive site around Comino. P31 was 52m patrol boat scuttled 2009 off Comino. Wreck is at 18-20m depth.
